channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump administration to cut California school funding over LGBT+ policies
item.description: California Senator Scott Wiener condemned Trump's decision to cut school funding in response to the state's LGBTQ+ policies in schools, stating that it is a form of cruelty towards LGBTQ kids and a distraction from Trump's own staff's security breach.
item.dc:creator: Adoreil Ayoubgoulan, Fox 5

item.title: Steph Curry scores a 3-book deal, starting with a 'personal reflection' on his life and career
item.description: Steph Curry has a deal with a Random House Publishing Group imprint that you could call a 3-point play. The NBA superstar has agreed to release three books through One World, beginning Sept. 9 with “Shot Ready," which Curry is calling a “personal reflection” on his life on and off the court.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump increasingly asks the Supreme Court to overrule judges blocking key parts of his agenda
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As losses mount in lower federal courts, President Donald Trump has returned to a tactic that he employed at the Supreme Court with remarkable success in his first term.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Amanda Knox's advice for American linked to Punta Cana missing persons case
item.description: Amanda Knox was a student studying abroad in Italy when she was accused of murdering friend Meredith Kercher. She spent nearly four years in an Italian prison before she was exonerated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kidnapped 6-year-old girl discovered during traffic stop: troopers
item.description: A trooper's discovery during a routine traffic stop last month led to the rescue of a 6-year-old girl who had been kidnapped for weeks, according to the Kansas Highway Patrol.
item.dc:creator: Barry Owens, KTLA 5

item.title: Delta plane, Air Force jet nearly crash in 'loss of separation' during Arlington National Cemetery flyover
item.description: A Delta plane carrying 131 passengers nearly collided with an Air Force jet in a 'loss of separation' Friday leading up to a flyover at Arlington National Cemetery.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State Department sends Congress letter saying it's taking over USAID
item.description: A source tells NewsNation a team trying to organize support for victims of the Myanmar earthquake was told to leave the office at midday.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Voice of America wins in court, for now, as judge blocks Trump administration from firing staff
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Voice of America can’t be silenced just yet. A federal judge on Friday halted the Trump administration’s efforts to dismantle the eight-decade-old U.S. government-funded international news service, calling the move a “classic case of arbitrary and capricious decision making.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stolen truck pursuit ends with violent, multi-vehicle pileup in Southern California
item.description: One of the more dangerous and destructive police pursuits in recent memory unfolded on live television in south Los Angeles County Friday afternoon, involving a driver in a white stolen pickup truck.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Authorities find skeletal remains at home of Georgia man who died in Hawaii
item.description: Investigators at the medical examiner's office do not suspect any foul play, a representative told Nexstar.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Joe Harris, believed to be the oldest surviving WWII paratrooper, has died
item.description: Sgt. Joe Harris, believed to be the oldest surviving World War II paratrooper and a member of the U.S. Army’s first all-Black parachute infantry battalion, has died. He was 108. Harris died March 15 in a hospital in Los Angeles surrounded by family, grandson Ashton Pittman told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stryker armored vehicles deploy to US-Mexico border under Trump administration
item.description: The Pentagon is supporting U.S. Customs and Border Protection by deploying eight Stryker vehicles to deter and detect migrants at the U.S.-Mexico border near El Paso.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Baby's corpse, busted casket found on grounds of West Virginia cemetery
item.description: A baby's corpse and a busted casket were found at a cemetery in Salt Rock, West Virginia, on Thursday evening, according to the Cabell County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Barren, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk to hand out $1M checks ahead of judicial race in Wisconsin
item.description: Elon Musk said he would be traveling to Wisconsin before the Supreme Court race to speak with voters and give out $1 million checks to several people who have already cast ballots.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Baby's corpse, busted casket found on grounds of West Virginia cemetery
item.description: A baby's corpse and a busted casket were found at a cemetery in Salt Rock, West Virginia, on Thursday evening, according to the Cabell County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Barren, KRON4

item.title: Texas hospital treating children with vitamin A toxicity: Reports
item.description: The CDC notes that it is not a substitute for vaccination, and warns that overusing Vitamin A can lead to toxicity.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: The top-rated seafood restaurant in every state, according to Yelp
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Consumer sentiment falls to lowest level in over two years
item.description: The University of Michigan's Survey of Consumers found a nearly 12% drop in consumer sentiment to 57 in March.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: This is the top-rated seafood restaurant in California, according to Yelp
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner. Knowing this, the analysts at Yelp have sought to identify the top-rated seafood restaurants in each state.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Newsom-backed San Francisco speed camera program fines certain drivers more than others
item.description: San Francisco launched a pilot program approved by California Gov. Gavin Newsom that allows the city to issue fines to speeding drivers based on income.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) warned in a speech Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline if they agree to a deal that achieves only “illusory peace.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: A mayor's lawsuit shut down a newspaper over fake stories. Now he faces corruption allegations
item.description: EVERETT, Mass. (AP) — For years, the mayor of a Boston suburb dreaded Wednesdays. That was the day when a local weekly would publish shocking allegations that he was on the take, sexually harassing women or under investigation by the FBI.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell warned Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: A second South Carolina death row inmate chooses execution by firing squad
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina death row inmate on Friday chose execution by firing squad, just five weeks after the state carried out its first death by bullets. Mikal Mahdi, who pleaded guilty to murder for killing a police officer in 2004, is scheduled to be executed April 11.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 4 dead after severe storms in the South Texas-Mexico border region and hundreds rescued
item.description: Drenching rain along the Texas-Mexico border has let up but rescues are still ongoing after severe storms trapped residents in their homes, forced drivers to abandon their vehicles on flooded roads and shut down an airport.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked agents handcuffing her
item.description: Rumeysa Ozturk's whereabouts are unknown and no known charges have been filed against her, her lawyer said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Million-dollar lottery ticket was nearly thrown out, winner says
item.description: “I was actually kind of going to throw them away, because I said ‘I’ve never won the lottery,’” said Joaquin Vaughn Redo.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Department goes to bat for Trump in hush money case
item.description: The Justice Department went to bat for President Trump in his hush money criminal case Wednesday, urging for the case to be moved so he can attempt to toss his guilty verdict on presidential immunity grounds.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump places 25% tariff on imported autos, expecting to raise $100 billion in tax revenues
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the tariffs would be detailed at a 4 p.m. EST news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NATO issues new statement on fate of 4 missing Fort Stewart soldiers
item.description: WIESBADEN, Germany (WSAV) — The NATO Acting spokesperson said that the Secretary General was referring to news articles and did not confirm the death of four U.S. Army 3rd Infantry Division soldiers reported missing in Lithuania Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: San Diego passes first-in-the-nation policy to ban digital-only coupons at grocery stores
item.description: The San Diego City Council has passed the Grocery Pricing Transparency Ordinance, requiring grocers to offer both digital and paper coupons to all customers, promoting equal access to grocery savings opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kermit the Frog to give commencement address at University of Maryland
item.description: Prominent political figures and lawmakers on the commencement speaker circuit are about to have some friendly, froggy competition.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Two dozen Kohl's stores to shutter this weekend, including in California
item.description: Kohl's announced 27 underperforming stores would close before April. The final day for those locations is coming this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Dollar Tree sells Family Dollar for $1 billion
item.description: Dollar Tree has agreed to sell Family Dollar for $1 billion, ending the short-lived merger deal and allowing both companies to focus on their core operations.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court for OK to cut teacher-training money as part of anti-DEI push
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Wednesday asked the Supreme Court to allow it to cut hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LDS church applies for smaller temple in Fairview, Texas, after reaching agreement; new rendering released
item.description: The LDS church says it has filed a new application for a controversial temple in Fairview, Texas, after reaching an agreement with the town.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4
